subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Amino Acid Fundamentals
content_to_test: Test understanding of the absolute configuration at the alpha position of amino acids, their behavior as dipolar ions (zwitterions), and their classification as acidic, basic, hydrophobic, or hydrophilic.

subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Amino Acid Reactions
content_to_test: Test knowledge of key amino acid reactions, including sulfur linkage formation for cysteine and cystine, the formation of peptide linkages in polypeptides and proteins, and the process of peptide hydrolysis.

subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Primary and Secondary Protein Structure
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the primary structure of proteins (amino acid sequence) and the secondary structure of proteins (alpha-helices and beta-pleated sheets).

subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Tertiary and Quaternary Protein Structure
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the tertiary structure of proteins, including the specific role of proline, cystine (disulfide bonds), and hydrophobic bonding, as well as the characteristics and significance of quaternary protein structure.

subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Protein Conformational Stability
content_to_test: Test understanding of the factors contributing to protein conformational stability, specifically denaturing and folding processes, hydrophobic interactions, and the role of the solvation layer (entropy) in protein folding.

subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Protein Separation Techniques
content_to_test: Test the principles and applications of protein separation techniques, focusing on the concept of the isoelectric point (pI) and the mechanism of electrophoresis.

subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Enzymatic Catalysis and Specificity
content_to_test: Test understanding of the function of enzymes in catalyzing biological reactions, their classification by reaction type, their role in the reduction of activation energy, and the basis of substrate specificity.

subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Enzyme-Substrate Models
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the theoretical models of enzyme-substrate interaction, specifically the Active Site Model and the Induced-Fit Model, as well as the broader mechanism of catalysis.

subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Enzyme Cofactors and Environment
content_to_test: Test knowledge of enzyme cofactors and coenzymes (including water-soluble vitamins) and how local environmental conditions (like pH and temperature) affect overall enzyme activity.

subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Enzyme Kinetics
content_to_test: Test understanding of enzyme kinetics, including general catalysis velocity principles, the assumptions and equations of Michaelis-Menten kinetics, and the concept of cooperativity.

subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Enzyme Inhibition and Feedback
content_to_test: Test knowledge of feedback regulation and the mechanisms and kinetic effects of different types of enzyme inhibition: competitive, noncompetitive, mixed, and uncompetitive inhibition.

subcategory: 1A: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
title: Regulatory Enzymes
content_to_test: Test understanding of regulatory enzymes, including the structural and functional features of allosteric enzymes, covalently modified enzymes, and zymogens.

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: Nucleic Acid Structure
content_to_test: Test knowledge of nucleotide and nucleoside composition, the sugar-phosphate backbone, pyrimidine and purine residues, and the Watson-Crick model of the DNA double helix including base pairing specificity.

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: DNA Replication Mechanism
content_to_test: Test understanding of the mechanism of DNA replication, including the separation of strands, specific coupling of free nucleic acids, the semiconservative nature of replication, origins of replication, and replicating the ends of DNA molecules.

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: Genetic Code and Central Dogma
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the Central Dogma, the triplet code, codon-anticodon relationships, the degenerate nature of the code, wobble pairing, missense/nonsense codons, and initiation/termination codons.

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: Transcription Mechanism
content_to_test: Test understanding of the mechanism of transcription, including the specific roles of messenger RNA (mRNA), transfer RNA (tRNA), and ribosomal RNA (rRNA).

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: mRNA Processing in Eukaryotes
content_to_test: Test knowledge of post-transcriptional mRNA processing in eukaryotes, including the roles of introns, exons, spliceosomes, snRNPs, and snRNAs, as well as the functional and evolutionary importance of introns.

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: Translation and Post-Translational Modification
content_to_test: Test understanding of the translation process, including the structure and function of ribosomes, roles of initiation and termination co-factors, and subsequent post-translational modifications of proteins.

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: Eukaryotic Chromosome Organization
content_to_test: Test knowledge of eukaryotic chromosome organization, focusing on chromosomal proteins, single copy versus repetitive DNA, supercoiling, heterochromatin versus euchromatin, and the structures of telomeres and centromeres.

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: Gene Expression Control in Prokaryotes
content_to_test: Test understanding of the control of gene expression in prokaryotes, specifically focusing on the Operon Concept (Jacob-Monod Model), mechanisms of gene repression, and positive control in bacteria.

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: Gene Expression Control in Eukaryotes
content_to_test: Test knowledge of eukaryotic gene expression control, including transcriptional regulation via DNA binding proteins and transcription factors, gene amplification and duplication, and post-transcriptional control.

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: Epigenetics and Cancer Biology
content_to_test: Test understanding of cancer as a failure of normal cellular controls (oncogenes, tumor suppressor genes), as well as epigenetic controls like regulation of chromatin structure, DNA methylation, and the role of noncoding RNAs.

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: Recombinant DNA: Cloning and Libraries
content_to_test: Test knowledge of recombinant DNA technology focusing on gene cloning, the use of restriction enzymes, the creation of DNA libraries, generation of cDNA, hybridization, and expressing cloned genes.

subcategory: 1B: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
title: DNA Analysis Techniques
content_to_test: Test understanding of key laboratory techniques in biotechnology, including the polymerase chain reaction (PCR), gel electrophoresis, Southern blotting, DNA sequencing, and methods for analyzing gene expression or determining gene function.

subcategory: 1C: Transmission of heritable information from generation to generation and the processes that increase genetic diversity
title: Mendelian Concepts Fundamentals
content_to_test: Test knowledge of classical Mendelian concepts, including phenotype and genotype, gene and locus definitions, single and multiple alleles, homozygosity and heterozygosity, wild-type, recessiveness, complete dominance, co-dominance, incomplete dominance, leakage, penetrance, expressivity, and hybridization viability.

subcategory: 1C: Transmission of heritable information from generation to generation and the processes that increase genetic diversity
title: Meiosis and Genetic Variability
content_to_test: Test understanding of meiosis and factors affecting genetic variability, including the segregation of genes, independent assortment, linkage, recombination, single and double crossovers, synaptonemal complex, tetrad formation, sex-linked characteristics, sex determination, and extranuclear inheritance.

subcategory: 1C: Transmission of heritable information from generation to generation and the processes that increase genetic diversity
title: Genetic Mutations
content_to_test: Test knowledge of mutations, including general concepts of DNA sequence errors, types of mutations (random, translation/transcription errors, base substitutions, inversions, additions, deletions, translocations, mispairings), advantageous vs. deleterious impacts, inborn errors of metabolism, and the relationship of mutagens to carcinogens.

subcategory: 1C: Transmission of heritable information from generation to generation and the processes that increase genetic diversity
title: Analytic Methods in Genetics
content_to_test: Test understanding of analytical methods in population and classical genetics, specifically the Hardy-Weinberg Principle, testcross applications (parental, F1, and F2 generations), gene mapping using crossover frequencies, and statistical methods (biometry).

subcategory: 1C: Transmission of heritable information from generation to generation and the processes that increase genetic diversity
title: Evolution and Natural Selection
content_to_test: Test knowledge of evolutionary theory, including natural selection, fitness, selection by differential reproduction, natural and group selection concepts, speciation, polymorphism, adaptation, specialization, inbreeding, outbreeding, bottlenecks, and evolutionary time measured by genome changes.

subcategory: 1D: Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism
title: Principles of Bioenergetics
content_to_test: Test understanding of the principles of bioenergetics and thermodynamics, focusing on the equilibrium constant (Keq), its relationship with free energy (delta G), Le Chatelier's Principle, endothermic vs. exothermic reactions, and spontaneous reactions linked to standard free energy.

subcategory: 1D: Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism
title: Phosphoryl Group Transfers and Redox
content_to_test: Test knowledge of ATP hydrolysis (delta G << 0), ATP group transfers, and biological oxidation-reduction including half-reactions and soluble electron carriers (such as flavoproteins).

subcategory: 1D: Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism
title: Carbohydrate Structure and Function
content_to_test: Test knowledge of carbohydrates, including nomenclature, classification, and common names. Focus on absolute configuration, cyclic structure, conformations of hexoses, epimers, anomers, hydrolysis of glycosidic linkages, and the functions of key monosaccharides, disaccharides, and polysaccharides.

subcategory: 1D: Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism
title: Glycolysis and Fermentation
content_to_test: Test knowledge of glycolysis (aerobic) including substrates and products, feeder pathways such as glycogen and starch metabolism, and the process of fermentation (anaerobic glycolysis).

subcategory: 1D: Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism
title: Gluconeogenesis and Pentose Phosphate Pathway
content_to_test: Test knowledge of gluconeogenesis and the pentose phosphate pathway, including their substrates, products, and their net molecular and energetic results within the broader context of cellular respiration.

subcategory: 1D: Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism
title: Metabolic Regulation and Glycogen
content_to_test: Test understanding of metabolic regulation principles, focusing on the maintenance of a dynamic steady state, regulation of glycolysis and gluconeogenesis, metabolism of glycogen (including regulation of synthesis and breakdown), allosteric and hormonal control, and the analysis of metabolic control.

subcategory: 1D: Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism
title: Citric Acid Cycle
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the Citric Acid Cycle, including acetyl-CoA production, specific reactions of the cycle with their substrates and products, overall regulation of the cycle, and the net molecular and energetic results of these respiration processes.

subcategory: 1D: Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism
title: Fatty Acid Metabolism
content_to_test: Test understanding of fatty acid metabolism, including the structural description of fatty acids, processes of digestion, mobilization, and transport of fats, the oxidation of saturated and unsaturated fatty acids, and the formation and role of ketone bodies.

subcategory: 1D: Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism
title: Lipid and Protein Anabolism
content_to_test: Test knowledge of lipid and protein anabolism, specifically the anabolism of fats, nontemplate synthesis (biosynthesis of lipids and polysaccharides), and the fundamental metabolism of proteins.

subcategory: 1D: Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism
title: Oxidative Phosphorylation
content_to_test: Test understanding of oxidative phosphorylation and the electron transport chain (ETC). Topics include ETC substrates and products, electron transfer in mitochondria (NADH, NADPH, flavoproteins, cytochromes), ATP synthase, chemiosmotic coupling, proton motive force, and the roles of mitochondria in apoptosis and oxidative stress.

subcategory: 1D: Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism
title: Hormonal Regulation of Metabolism
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the hormonal regulation and integration of metabolism, including higher-level integration of hormone structure and function, tissue-specific metabolism, hormonal regulation of fuel metabolism, and the regulation of body mass and obesity.

subcategory: 2A: Assemblies of molecules, cells, and groups of cells within single cellular and multicellular organisms
title: Plasma Membrane Composition and Dynamics
content_to_test: Test understanding of the plasma membrane, including its general function in cell containment, composition of lipid components (phospholipids, steroids, waxes), protein components, the fluid mosaic model, and membrane dynamics.

subcategory: 2A: Assemblies of molecules, cells, and groups of cells within single cellular and multicellular organisms
title: Solute Transport and Thermodynamics
content_to_test: Test thermodynamic considerations of transport, osmosis, colligative properties (osmotic pressure), passive transport, active transport, and the sodium/potassium pump.

subcategory: 2A: Assemblies of molecules, cells, and groups of cells within single cellular and multicellular organisms
title: Membrane Dynamics and Intercellular Junctions
content_to_test: Test knowledge of membrane channels, membrane potential, membrane receptors, exocytosis, endocytosis, and intercellular junctions (gap junctions, tight junctions, desmosomes).

subcategory: 2A: Assemblies of molecules, cells, and groups of cells within single cellular and multicellular organisms
title: Eukaryotic Organelles
content_to_test: Test understanding of the defining characteristics of eukaryotic cells and membrane-bound organelles. Topics include the nucleus (compartmentalization, nucleolus, nuclear envelope, pores), mitochondria (ATP production, inner/outer membrane structure, self-replication), lysosomes, endoplasmic reticulum (rough vs smooth, roles in biosynthesis), Golgi apparatus, and peroxisomes.

subcategory: 2A: Assemblies of molecules, cells, and groups of cells within single cellular and multicellular organisms
title: Cytoskeleton Elements
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the eukaryotic cytoskeleton, including its general function in support and movement, the composition and role of microfilaments (cleavage and contractility), microtubules (support, transport, cilia, flagella, centrioles), and intermediate filaments.

subcategory: 2A: Assemblies of molecules, cells, and groups of cells within single cellular and multicellular organisms
title: Eukaryotic Tissue Types
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the tissues formed from eukaryotic cells, differentiating between the characteristics and functions of epithelial cells and connective tissue cells.

subcategory: 2B: The structure, growth, physiology, and genetics of prokaryotes and viruses
title: Prokaryotic Classification and Structure
content_to_test: Test understanding of cell theory and the classification and structure of prokaryotic cells. Topics include the history and impact of cell theory, prokaryotic domains (Archaea, Bacteria), major bacterial shapes (bacilli, spirilli, cocci), and distinguishing features like the lack of nuclear membrane and organelles, the presence of a cell wall, and flagellar propulsion.

subcategory: 2B: The structure, growth, physiology, and genetics of prokaryotes and viruses
title: Prokaryotic Growth and Genetics
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the growth, physiology, and genetics of prokaryotic cells. Topics include reproduction by fission, exponential growth, anaerobic/aerobic variants, parasitic/symbiotic relationships, chemotaxis, plasmids, transformation, conjugation, and transposons.

subcategory: 2B: The structure, growth, physiology, and genetics of prokaryotes and viruses
title: Virus Structure and Life Cycle
content_to_test: Test understanding of virus structure and the viral life cycle. Topics include structural characteristics (enveloped/nonenveloped, DNA/RNA content, size relative to cells), the lack of organelles/nucleus, generalized phage and animal virus life cycles, host penetration, host machinery hijacking, self-assembly, transduction, retroviruses (HIV), and subviral particles (prions, viroids).

subcategory: 2C: Processes of cell division, differentiation, and specialization
title: Mitosis and Cell Cycle Control
content_to_test: Test knowledge of mitosis and the cell cycle. Topics include mitotic phases, mitotic structures (centrioles, asters, spindles, chromatids, centromeres, kinetochores), chromosome movement mechanisms, phases of the cell cycle (G0, G1, S, G2, M), growth arrest, cell cycle control, and the loss of controls in cancer (oncogenes, apoptosis).

subcategory: 2C: Processes of cell division, differentiation, and specialization
title: Gametogenesis and Reproductive Sequence
content_to_test: Test understanding of the reproductive system, including gametogenesis by meiosis, differences between ovum and sperm (formation, morphology, relative genetic contributions), and the reproductive sequence (fertilization, implantation, development, birth).

subcategory: 2C: Processes of cell division, differentiation, and specialization
title: Early Embryogenesis
content_to_test: Test knowledge of early embryogenesis, including fertilization, cleavage, blastula formation, gastrulation, the first cell movements, formation of primary germ layers (endoderm, mesoderm, ectoderm) and their major derived structures, neurulation, neural crest, and environment-gene interactions during development.

subcategory: 2C: Processes of cell division, differentiation, and specialization
title: Mechanisms of Development
content_to_test: Test understanding of the mechanisms of development, including cell specialization (determination, differentiation, tissue types), cell-cell communication, cell migration, pluripotency of stem cells, gene regulation in development, programmed cell death, regenerative capacity, and senescence/aging.

subcategory: 3A: Structure and functions of the nervous and endocrine systems and ways these systems coordinate the organ systems
title: Nervous System Organization
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the structure and function of the nervous system, including high-level control and integration of body systems, sensor and effector neurons, sympathetic and parasympathetic antagonistic control, reflexes (feedback loop, reflex arc), and the role of the spinal cord and supraspinal circuits.

subcategory: 3A: Structure and functions of the nervous and endocrine systems and ways these systems coordinate the organ systems
title: Nerve Cell Structure and Function
content_to_test: Test understanding of nerve cell structure and function, including the cell body, dendrites, axon structure (myelin sheath, Schwann cells, Nodes of Ranvier), impulse propagation, synapses, neurotransmitters, resting potential, action potential (threshold, all-or-none, sodium-potassium pump, summation/frequency), and glial cells.

subcategory: 3A: Structure and functions of the nervous and endocrine systems and ways these systems coordinate the organ systems
title: Neuro-Electrochemistry and Biosignaling
content_to_test: Test knowledge of electrochemistry and biosignaling in the nervous system. Topics include the use of the Nernst equation for concentration cells, gated ion channels (voltage-gated and ligand-gated), receptor enzymes, and G protein-coupled receptors.

subcategory: 3A: Structure and functions of the nervous and endocrine systems and ways these systems coordinate the organ systems
title: Endocrine System and Hormones
content_to_test: Test understanding of the endocrine system's hormones and their sources, including the definitions of endocrine glands and hormones, specific chemical control at the cell/tissue/organ level, major endocrine glands and their products, major types of hormones, and neuroendocrinology (the relation between neurons and hormonal systems).

subcategory: 3A: Structure and functions of the nervous and endocrine systems and ways these systems coordinate the organ systems
title: Mechanisms of Hormone Action
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the mechanisms of hormone action, including cellular mechanisms, transport of hormones via blood supply, specificity of hormones to target tissues, integration with the nervous system via feedback control, and regulation by second messengers.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Respiratory System Functions and Structure
content_to_test: Test understanding of the general functions of the respiratory system, including gas exchange, thermoregulation, and protection against disease via particulate filtration (nasal hairs, mucus-cilia system). Also test the structure of the lungs and alveoli.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Breathing Mechanisms
content_to_test: Test knowledge of breathing mechanisms, including the functional roles of the diaphragm and rib cage, differential pressure generation, and the effects of lung resiliency and surface tension.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Gas Exchange and pH Control
content_to_test: Test understanding of alveolar gas exchange, including the mechanisms of diffusion, differential partial pressure, and Henry's Law. Also test the respiratory system's role in pH control and its regulation by nervous control (specifically CO2 sensitivity).

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Circulatory System and Heart Anatomy
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the circulatory system's broad functions (circulation of oxygen, nutrients, hormones, ions, fluids, waste removal, and thermoregulation), as well as the structure and function of the four-chambered heart, including endothelial cells and the concepts of systolic and diastolic pressure.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Blood Vessels and Fluid Dynamics
content_to_test: Test understanding of the arterial and venous systems (arteries, arterioles, venules, veins), focusing on structural and functional differences, pressure and flow characteristics, capillary beds, mechanisms of gas and solute exchange, mechanisms of heat exchange, and the source of peripheral resistance.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Blood Composition and Transport
content_to_test: Test knowledge of blood composition (plasma, chemicals, blood cells), erythrocyte production and destruction (role of spleen and bone marrow), regulation of plasma volume, coagulation and clotting mechanisms, and oxygen/carbon dioxide transport by hemoglobin (including oxygen affinity and nervous/endocrine control).

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Lymphatic System
content_to_test: Test understanding of the structure and major functions of the lymphatic system, including the equalization of fluid distribution, transport of proteins and large glycerides, production of lymphocytes for immune reactions, and the return of materials to the blood.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Immune System Cells and Organs
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the immune system's cellular organization, specifically the differences between innate (nonspecific) and adaptive (specific) immunity. Test the roles of adaptive cells (T-lymphocytes, B-lymphocytes), innate cells (macrophages, phagocytes), and related tissues (bone marrow, spleen, thymus, lymph nodes).

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Antigens, Antibodies, and Recognition
content_to_test: Test understanding of the concepts of antigens and antibodies, including antigen presentation, clonal selection, antigen-antibody recognition, the structural components of the antibody molecule, the mechanism of recognizing self vs. nonself (and autoimmune diseases), and the major histocompatibility complex (MHC).

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Upper Digestive Tract and Accessory Organs
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the upper digestive system and accessory organs. Topics include saliva function, esophageal transport, stomach roles (storage, churning, low pH, gastric juice, enzyme production), liver structure and function (bile production, blood glucose regulation, detoxification), gallbladder storage, and pancreatic enzyme production.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Lower Digestive Tract and GI Control
content_to_test: Test understanding of the lower digestive tract and system control. Topics include the small intestine (absorption, villi structure/function, enzyme production, stomach acid neutralization, subdivisions), large intestine (water absorption, bacterial flora), rectum, and the muscular (peristalsis) and endocrine/nervous (enteric nervous system) control of digestion.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Excretory System: Homeostasis and Gross Anatomy
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the excretory system's roles in homeostasis (blood pressure, osmoregulation, acid-base balance, removal of soluble nitrogenous waste) and the gross structure of the kidney (cortex, medulla).

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Nephron Structure and Function
content_to_test: Test the detailed functional anatomy of the nephron, including the glomerulus, Bowman's capsule, proximal tubule, loop of Henle, distal tubule, and collecting duct.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Urine Formation and Osmoregulation
content_to_test: Test understanding of the physiological formation of urine, including glomerular filtration, secretion and reabsorption of solutes, concentration of urine via the counter-current multiplier mechanism, storage/elimination (ureter, bladder, urethra), and general osmoregulation via capillary reabsorption.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Reproductive System Structure and Function
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the male and female reproductive systems, including structures (gonads, genitalia) and their differences, hormonal control of reproduction, male and female sexual development, the female reproductive cycle, pregnancy, parturition, lactation, and the integration of these processes with nervous control.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Muscle System Organization
content_to_test: Test understanding of the muscle system's overall functions (support, mobility, peripheral circulatory assistance, thermoregulation/shivering reflex) and the structural differences between the three basic muscle types: striated, smooth, and cardiac. Also test oxygen debt, fatigue, and nervous control (motor neurons, neuromuscular junction, sympathetic/parasympathetic innervation).

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Muscle Cell Contraction Mechanisms
content_to_test: Test knowledge of specialized muscle cells, specifically structural characteristics of different muscle types, the abundance of mitochondria in red muscle cells, the organization of contractile elements (actin, myosin, crossbridges, sliding filament model), sarcomere structure (I and A bands, M and Z lines, H zone), the presence of troponin and tropomyosin, and calcium regulation of contraction.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Skeletal System Structure and Function
content_to_test: Test understanding of the skeletal system's functions (structural rigidity, support, calcium storage, physical protection) and structure (specialization of bone types, joint structures, endoskeleton vs. exoskeleton, microscopic bone structure, calcium-protein matrix, cellular composition). Also test the structure/function of cartilage, ligaments, tendons, and endocrine control of bone.

subcategory: 3B: Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems
title: Skin System
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the skin system's structure (layer differentiation, cell types, relative impermeability to water) and functions in homeostasis, osmoregulation, and thermoregulation (hair, erectile musculature, fat layer, sweat glands, vasoconstriction/vasodilation). Also test its role in physical protection and related hormonal controls.

subcategory: 4A: Translational motion, forces, work, energy, and equilibrium in living systems
title: Translational Motion
content_to_test: Test knowledge of translational motion, including physics units and dimensions, vector definition and components, vector addition, definitions and calculations of speed, average and instantaneous velocity, and acceleration.

subcategory: 4A: Translational motion, forces, work, energy, and equilibrium in living systems
title: Forces and Equilibrium
content_to_test: Test understanding of forces and equilibrium, focusing on Newton's First Law (inertia), Newton's Second Law (F=ma), Newton's Third Law, static and kinetic friction, center of mass, vector analysis of forces acting on a point object, and the calculation of torques and lever arms.

subcategory: 4A: Translational motion, forces, work, energy, and equilibrium in living systems
title: Work and Energy
content_to_test: Test knowledge of work and energy in point object systems. Topics include work done by a constant force, mechanical advantage, the Work-Kinetic Energy Theorem, conservative forces, formulas for kinetic energy and potential energy (gravitational and spring), the conservation of energy principle, and power calculation.

subcategory: 4A: Translational motion, forces, work, energy, and equilibrium in living systems
title: Periodic Motion
content_to_test: Test understanding of periodic motion, specifically identifying and utilizing amplitude, frequency, phase, and the characteristics of transverse and longitudinal waves (including wavelength and propagation speed).

subcategory: 4B: Importance of fluids for the circulation of blood, gas movement, and gas exchange
title: Fluid Properties and Hydrostatics
content_to_test: Test knowledge of static fluid properties, including density, specific gravity, buoyancy, Archimedes' Principle, hydrostatic pressure definitions, Pascal's Law, and the relationship between hydrostatic pressure and depth.

subcategory: 4B: Importance of fluids for the circulation of blood, gas movement, and gas exchange
title: Fluid Dynamics Principles
content_to_test: Test understanding of pure fluid dynamics, including viscosity (Poiseuille Flow), the continuity equation, turbulence, surface tension, Bernoulli's equation, the Venturi effect, and the pitot tube.

subcategory: 4B: Importance of fluids for the circulation of blood, gas movement, and gas exchange
title: Cardiovascular Fluid Dynamics
content_to_test: Test the physiological application of fluid models to the circulatory system, including arterial and venous pressure, flow characteristics, and the source of peripheral resistance.

subcategory: 4B: Importance of fluids for the circulation of blood, gas movement, and gas exchange
title: Ideal Gases
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the gas phase, including absolute temperature (Kelvin scale), pressure measurement via mercury barometer, molar volume at 0 degrees Celsius and 1 atm, and the definition and application of the Ideal Gas Law (including Boyle's Law, Charles' Law, and Avogadro's Law).

subcategory: 4B: Importance of fluids for the circulation of blood, gas movement, and gas exchange
title: Kinetic Molecular Theory and Real Gases
content_to_test: Test understanding of the Kinetic Molecular Theory of Gases, heat capacity at constant volume vs. constant pressure, Boltzmann's Constant, deviation of real gas behavior from the Ideal Gas Law (qualitatively and quantitatively via Van der Waals' Equation), partial pressure, mole fraction, and Dalton's Law.

subcategory: 4C: Electrochemistry and electrical circuits and their elements
title: Electrostatics Fundamentals
content_to_test: Test understanding of electrostatics, including the concepts of charge, conductors, charge conservation, insulators, Coulomb's Law, the definition and calculation of electric fields (including field lines and distribution), electrostatic energy, and electric potential at a point in space.

subcategory: 4C: Electrochemistry and electrical circuits and their elements
title: Circuit Elements: Resistance and Current
content_to_test: Test knowledge of basic circuit elements focusing on current (sign conventions, units), electromotive force, voltage, and resistance. Include Ohm's Law, calculation of resistors in series and parallel, resistivity, conductivity (metallic and electrolytic), and the use of meters.

subcategory: 4C: Electrochemistry and electrical circuits and their elements
title: Circuit Elements: Capacitance
content_to_test: Test understanding of capacitance within electrical circuits, specifically focusing on parallel plate capacitors, calculating the energy of a charged capacitor, computing equivalent capacitance for capacitors in series and parallel, and the function of dielectrics.

subcategory: 4C: Electrochemistry and electrical circuits and their elements
title: Magnetism
content_to_test: Test knowledge of magnetism, including the definition of the magnetic field B, the motion of charged particles in magnetic fields, and the calculation and application of the Lorentz force.

subcategory: 4C: Electrochemistry and electrical circuits and their elements
title: Electrolytic Cells and Electrolysis
content_to_test: Test understanding of electrolytic cells, the process of electrolysis, the specific roles and reactions at the anode and cathode, electrolytes, and the application of Faraday's Law relating current to the amount of elements deposited or gas liberated.

subcategory: 4C: Electrochemistry and electrical circuits and their elements
title: Galvanic Cells and Batteries
content_to_test: Test knowledge of Galvanic (Voltaic) cells and concentration cells, including half-reactions, reduction potentials, calculating cell potential, determining the direction of electron flow, and specific battery types such as lead-storage and nickel-cadmium batteries.

subcategory: 4C: Electrochemistry and electrical circuits and their elements
title: Nerve Cell as an Electrical Circuit
content_to_test: Test understanding of the physical properties of the nerve cell acting as an electrical circuit, focusing on the insulation of the axon by the myelin sheath (Schwann cells) and the propagation of the nerve impulse along the axon via the Nodes of Ranvier.

subcategory: 4D: How light and sound interact with matter
title: Properties of Sound
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the physical properties of sound, including its production, relative speed in solids, liquids, and gases, intensity (decibel units, log scale), attenuation (damping), the Doppler Effect (moving source/observer, reflection from a moving object), pitch, resonance in pipes and strings, ultrasound, and shock waves.

subcategory: 4D: How light and sound interact with matter
title: Electromagnetic Radiation and Light
content_to_test: Test understanding of light and electromagnetic radiation. Topics include interference (Young's double-slit experiment), thin films, diffraction (grating, single-slit, X-ray), polarization (linear and circular), properties of EM radiation (constant velocity c, perpendicular electric and magnetic fields), the EM spectrum, photon energy (E=hf), and the visual spectrum.

subcategory: 4D: How light and sound interact with matter
title: Absorption Spectra and Spectroscopy
content_to_test: Test knowledge of molecular structure and absorption spectra, including the Infrared region (intramolecular vibrations, fingerprint region), Visible region (complementary colors, structural changes/indicators), Ultraviolet region (pi-electrons, conjugated systems), and NMR spectroscopy (protons in a magnetic field, equivalent protons, spin-spin splitting).

subcategory: 4D: How light and sound interact with matter
title: Geometrical Optics: Reflection and Refraction
content_to_test: Test understanding of geometrical optics focusing on reflection from plane surfaces (angle of incidence equals angle of reflection), refraction (refractive index, Snell's law), dispersion (change of index of refraction with wavelength), and the conditions for total internal reflection.

subcategory: 4D: How light and sound interact with matter
title: Geometrical Optics: Mirrors and Lenses
content_to_test: Test knowledge of spherical mirrors (center of curvature, focal length, real/virtual images) and thin lenses (converging/diverging, lens formula with sign conventions, lens strength/diopters, combinations, aberration). Also test the application of these principles to optical instruments, including the human eye.

subcategory: 4E: Atoms, nuclear decay, electronic structure, and atomic chemical behavior
title: The Atomic Nucleus and Radioactivity
content_to_test: Test understanding of the atomic nucleus, including atomic number, atomic weight, neutrons, protons, isotopes, nuclear forces, binding energy, radioactive decay (alpha, beta, gamma), half-life (exponential decay, semi-log plots), and the use of mass spectroscopy/mass spectrometers.

subcategory: 4E: Atoms, nuclear decay, electronic structure, and atomic chemical behavior
title: Electronic Structure of the Atom
content_to_test: Test knowledge of electronic structure, including the orbital structure of the hydrogen atom, principal quantum number n, electrons per orbital, ground vs excited states, absorption/emission line spectra, Pauli Exclusion Principle, paramagnetism, diamagnetism, conventional notation, the Bohr atom, Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle, effective nuclear charge, and the photoelectric effect.

subcategory: 4E: Atoms, nuclear decay, electronic structure, and atomic chemical behavior
title: Periodic Table Classifications
content_to_test: Test understanding of the classification of elements in the periodic table, specifically identifying and understanding the characteristics of alkali metals, alkaline earth metals, halogens, noble gases, transition metals, representative elements, metals vs. nonmetals, and the oxygen group.

subcategory: 4E: Atoms, nuclear decay, electronic structure, and atomic chemical behavior
title: Periodic Trends
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the periodic variations of chemical properties with group and row. Topics include valence electrons, first and second ionization energy, electron affinity, electronegativity, and the relative sizes of atoms and ions based on electron shells.

subcategory: 4E: Atoms, nuclear decay, electronic structure, and atomic chemical behavior
title: Stoichiometry and Chemical Equations
content_to_test: Test understanding of stoichiometry, including molecular weight, empirical vs. molecular formulas, percent mass composition, the mole concept (Avogadro's number), density, oxidation numbers, common oxidizing/reducing agents, disproportionation reactions, and the description of chemical reactions via balancing equations (including redox), limiting reactants, and theoretical yields.

subcategory: 5A: Unique nature of water and its solutions
title: Acid-Base Equilibria
content_to_test: Test knowledge of acid-base equilibria, including Bronsted-Lowry definitions, the ionization of water and the value of Kw (10^-14 at 25C, 1 atm), pH definition, conjugate acids/bases, strong vs. weak acids/bases, dissociation of weak acids/bases with or without added salt, hydrolysis of salts, pH calculation for salt solutions, the equilibrium constants Ka and Kb (and pKa/pKb), and the definition, concepts, and titration curve influence of buffers.

subcategory: 5A: Unique nature of water and its solutions
title: Ions in Solutions
content_to_test: Test understanding of ions in solutions, including common names, formulas, and charges for familiar anions and cations (e.g., ammonium, phosphate, sulfate), as well as the concept of hydration and the hydronium ion.

subcategory: 5A: Unique nature of water and its solutions
title: Solubility and Complex Ions
content_to_test: Test knowledge of solubility, including units of concentration (molarity), the solubility product constant equilibrium expression (Ksp), the common-ion effect and its use in lab separations, complex ion formation, and the relationship between solubility and pH.

subcategory: 5A: Unique nature of water and its solutions
title: Titration
content_to_test: Test understanding of titration principles, focusing on the use of indicators, the process of neutralization, the interpretation of titration curves, and redox titration.

subcategory: 5B: Nature of molecules and intermolecular interactions
title: Covalent Bonding and Molecular Structure
content_to_test: Test knowledge of covalent bonding and molecular shape. Topics include Lewis dot formulas, resonance structures, formal charge, Lewis acids/bases, partial ionic character/electronegativity/dipole moments, sigma and pi bonds, hybrid orbitals (sp3, sp2, sp) and their geometries, VSEPR theory for predicting shapes (e.g., NH3, H2O, CO2), delocalized electrons, and the effect of multiple bonding on bond length, energy, and rigidity.

subcategory: 5B: Nature of molecules and intermolecular interactions
title: Stereochemistry
content_to_test: Test understanding of stereochemistry for covalently bonded molecules, including structural isomers, stereoisomers (diastereomers, enantiomers, cis-trans), conformational isomers, polarization of light and specific rotation, and absolute/relative configurations including R/S and E/Z conventions.

subcategory: 5B: Nature of molecules and intermolecular interactions
title: Intermolecular Forces
content_to_test: Test knowledge of intermolecular forces in the liquid phase, specifically focusing on hydrogen bonding, dipole interactions, and Van der Waals (London dispersion) forces.

subcategory: 5C: Separation and purification methods
title: Extraction, Distillation, and Chromatography
content_to_test: Test understanding of standard laboratory separation and purification methods, including extraction (distribution between immiscible solvents), distillation, and the basic principles of chromatography (column, gas-liquid, high-pressure liquid, paper, and thin-layer).

subcategory: 5C: Separation and purification methods
title: Biomolecule and Enantiomer Separation
content_to_test: Test knowledge of advanced separation and purification techniques for specific molecules. Focus on separating peptides and proteins (via electrophoresis, quantitative analysis, size-exclusion, ion-exchange, and affinity chromatography) and the separation of racemic mixtures into enantiomers.

subcategory: 5D: Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically relevant molecules
title: Nucleotide Chemistry and RNA Structures
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the chemistry of nucleotides and nucleic acids beyond their fundamental structure. Focus on RNA structures, DNA/RNA distinctions at the chemical level, and the specific composition of nucleosides/nucleotides.

subcategory: 5D: Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically relevant molecules
title: Amino Acid Synthesis and Chemistry
content_to_test: Test understanding of the organic chemistry of amino acids. Topics include the synthesis of alpha-amino acids via the Strecker Synthesis and Gabriel Synthesis, as well as the chemical mechanisms of peptide linkage formation and peptide hydrolysis.

subcategory: 5D: Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically relevant molecules
title: Lipid Classification and Reactions
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the structural features and types of lipids, focusing on storage (triacyl glycerols, free fatty acids/saponification), structural roles (phospholipids, phosphatids, sphingolipids, waxes), and signaling/cofactor roles (fat-soluble vitamins, steroids, prostaglandins).

subcategory: 5D: Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically relevant molecules
title: Carbohydrate Chemistry
content_to_test: Test understanding of carbohydrate chemistry, particularly keto-enol tautomerism of monosaccharides, the mechanism of hydrolysis of the glycosidic linkage, and the specific chemical structures and conformations of key hexoses, disaccharides, and polysaccharides.

subcategory: 5D: Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically relevant molecules
title: Aldehydes and Ketones: Addition and Oxidation
content_to_test: Test knowledge of aldehydes and ketones, including their nomenclature, physical properties, oxidation of aldehydes, and nucleophilic addition reactions at the C=O bond (acetal, hemiacetal, imine, enamine, hydride reagents, and cyanohydrin formation).

subcategory: 5D: Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically relevant molecules
title: Enolate Chemistry and Aldol Condensation
content_to_test: Test understanding of reactions at adjacent positions to the carbonyl group (enolate chemistry). Topics include keto-enol tautomerism (alpha-racemization), the aldol condensation, retro-aldol reactions, kinetic vs. thermodynamic enolates, the acidity of the alpha-hydrogen (carbanions), and the effect of substituents/steric hindrance on C=O reactivity.

subcategory: 5D: Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically relevant molecules
title: Alcohols
content_to_test: Test knowledge of alcohols, including nomenclature, physical properties (acidity, hydrogen bonding), oxidation reactions, substitution reactions (SN1 or SN2), the protection of alcohols, and the preparation of mesylates and tosylates.

subcategory: 5D: Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically relevant molecules
title: Carboxylic Acids
content_to_test: Test understanding of carboxylic acids, including nomenclature, physical properties, carboxyl group reactions (formation of amides, esters, anhydrides; reduction; decarboxylation), and substitution reactions at the alpha-position.

subcategory: 5D: Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically relevant molecules
title: Acid Derivatives and Phenols
content_to_test: Test knowledge of acid derivatives (anhydrides, amides, esters), focusing on nomenclature, nucleophilic substitution, transesterification, amide hydrolysis, relative reactivity, steric/electronic effects, and lactam strain. Also test knowledge of phenols, including oxidation/reduction to hydroquinones/ubiquinones (biological 2e- redox centers), and polycyclic/heterocyclic aromatic compounds.

subcategory: 5E: Principles of chemical thermodynamics and kinetics
title: Thermochemistry and Thermodynamics
content_to_test: Test knowledge of thermodynamics in chemical reactions, including state functions, the Zeroth Law, the First Law (conservation of energy, PV diagram work), the Second Law (entropy), calorimetry, heat transfer (conduction, convection, radiation), endothermic/exothermic reactions, enthalpy, Hess's Law, bond dissociation energy, standard free energy, and spontaneous reactions.

subcategory: 5E: Principles of chemical thermodynamics and kinetics
title: Chemical Kinetics
content_to_test: Test understanding of chemical kinetics, including the dependence of reaction rate on reactant concentration, rate law, rate constant, reaction order, rate-determining step, activation energy, the activated complex (transition state), Arrhenius equation, the difference between kinetic and thermodynamic control, and the function of catalysts.

subcategory: 5E: Principles of chemical thermodynamics and kinetics
title: Chemical Equilibrium
content_to_test: Test knowledge of equilibrium in reversible chemical reactions. Topics include the Law of Mass Action, the Equilibrium Constant, the application of Le Chatelier's Principle, and the mathematical relationship between the equilibrium constant (Keq) and standard free energy (delta G).

subcategory: 6A: Sensing the environment
title: Sensory Processing Principles
content_to_test: Test understanding of sensory processing fundamentals, including absolute and difference thresholds, Weber's Law, signal detection theory, sensory adaptation, psychophysics, specific types of sensory receptors, and how sensory pathways route information to the brain.

subcategory: 6A: Sensing the environment
title: Vision and Hearing
content_to_test: Test knowledge of vision and hearing, focusing on the anatomical structure and physiological functions of the eye and ear, visual and auditory processing pathways in the brain, parallel processing, feature detection, and the mechanics of sensory reception by hair cells.

subcategory: 6A: Sensing the environment
title: Other Senses and Perception
content_to_test: Test understanding of the minor senses and overall perception. Topics include somatosensation (pain perception), taste (chemoreceptors), smell (olfactory cells, pheromones, olfactory pathways), kinesthetic sense, vestibular sense, bottom-up vs top-down processing, perceptual organization (depth, form, motion, constancy), and Gestalt principles.

subcategory: 6B: Making sense of the environment
title: Attention and Cognition
content_to_test: Test knowledge of attention (selective and divided) and cognition. Topics include the information-processing model, Piaget's stages of cognitive development, cognitive changes in late adulthood, cultural and biological influences on cognition, types of problem-solving, barriers to effective problem-solving (heuristics, biases, overconfidence, belief perseverance), and theories/variations of intelligence.

subcategory: 6B: Making sense of the environment
title: States of Consciousness
content_to_test: Test understanding of the states of consciousness, including alertness, sleep stages, sleep cycles, sleep and circadian rhythms, dreaming, sleep-wake disorders, hypnosis, meditation, and the types/effects of consciousness-altering drugs (including drug addiction and the brain's reward pathway).

subcategory: 6B: Making sense of the environment
title: Memory Encoding and Storage
content_to_test: Test knowledge of memory encoding processes and types of storage (sensory, working, long-term), as well as semantic networks and spreading activation.

subcategory: 6B: Making sense of the environment
title: Memory Retrieval and Forgetting
content_to_test: Test understanding of retrieval (recall, recognition, relearning, retrieval cues, role of emotion) and forgetting (decay, interference, aging, memory construction, and source monitoring).

subcategory: 6B: Making sense of the environment
title: Memory Dysfunctions and Biology
content_to_test: Test knowledge of memory dysfunctions (Alzheimer's disease, Korsakoff's syndrome) and the biological basis of memory and learning (neural plasticity, long-term potentiation).

subcategory: 6B: Making sense of the environment
title: Language Development and Brain Areas
content_to_test: Test understanding of language, including theories of language development (learning, nativist, interactionist), the influence of language on cognition, and the specific brain areas that control language and speech.

subcategory: 6C: Responding to the world
title: Theories and Components of Emotion
content_to_test: Test knowledge of emotion, focusing on its three components (cognitive, physiological, behavioral), the universal emotions, the adaptive role of emotion, theories of emotion (James-Lange, Cannon-Bard, Schachter-Singer), and the biological processes involved (brain regions, limbic system, autonomic nervous system, and physiological markers).

subcategory: 6C: Responding to the world
title: Stress and Stress Management
content_to_test: Test understanding of the nature of stress (appraisal, types of stressors like cataclysmic events or personal), the effects of stress on psychological functions, stress outcomes and responses (physiological, emotional, behavioral), and mechanisms for managing stress (exercise, relaxation, spirituality).

subcategory: 7A: Individual influences on behavior
title: Biological Bases of Behavior
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the biological bases of behavior. Topics include the nervous system (neurons, neurotransmitters, central/peripheral structure), brain structure (forebrain, midbrain, hindbrain, lateralization), endocrine system effects on behavior, behavioral genetics (genes, temperament, heredity, regulatory genes), and human physiological development (prenatal, motor, adolescence).

subcategory: 7A: Individual influences on behavior
title: Theories of Personality
content_to_test: Test understanding of the major theories of personality, including the psychoanalytic, humanistic, trait, social cognitive, biological, and behaviorist perspectives, as well as the situational approach to explaining behavior.

subcategory: 7A: Individual influences on behavior
title: Psychological Disorders
content_to_test: Test knowledge of psychological disorders, including biomedical vs. biopsychosocial approaches to understanding them, classification, and rates. Focus on specific types (anxiety, obsessive-compulsive, trauma, somatic symptom, bipolar, depressive, schizophrenia, dissociative, personality disorders) and the specific biological bases of schizophrenia, depression, Alzheimer's, and Parkinson's disease.

subcategory: 7A: Individual influences on behavior
title: Motivation and Attitudes
content_to_test: Test understanding of motivation and attitudes. Topics include factors influencing motivation (instinct, arousal, drives, needs), theories of motivation (drive reduction, incentive, cognitive, need-based), the components of attitudes (cognitive, affective, behavioral), the link between attitudes and behavior (foot-in-the-door, role-playing), and cognitive dissonance theory.

subcategory: 7B: Social processes that influence human behavior
title: Presence of Others and Individual Behavior
content_to_test: Test understanding of how the presence of others affects individual behavior, specifically focusing on social facilitation, deindividuation, the bystander effect, social loafing, social control, peer pressure, conformity, and obedience.

subcategory: 7B: Social processes that influence human behavior
title: Group Decision-Making and Social Norms
content_to_test: Test knowledge of group decision-making processes (group polarization, groupthink) and normative vs. nonnormative behavior. Topics include social norms, sanctions, folkways, mores, taboos, anomie, perspectives on deviance (differential association, labeling theory, strain theory), and collective behavior (fads, mass hysteria, riots).

subcategory: 7B: Social processes that influence human behavior
title: Socialization
content_to_test: Test understanding of socialization and the primary agents of socialization, including the family, mass media, peers, and the workplace.

subcategory: 7C: Attitude and behavior change
title: Habituation and Associative Learning
content_to_test: Test knowledge of habituation, dishabituation, and associative learning. Topics include classical conditioning (neutral/conditioned/unconditioned stimuli and responses, acquisition, extinction, spontaneous recovery, generalization, discrimination) and operant conditioning (shaping, extinction, types of reinforcement, reinforcement schedules, punishment, escape/avoidance learning). Also test the biological and cognitive processes affecting associative learning.

subcategory: 7C: Attitude and behavior change
title: Observational Learning and Attitude Change
content_to_test: Test understanding of observational learning (modeling, mirror neurons, vicarious emotions) and theories of attitude/behavior change, specifically the elaboration likelihood model, social cognitive theory, and factors affecting attitude change (message characteristics, target characteristics, social factors).

subcategory: 8A: Self-identity
title: Self-Concept and Identity Formation
content_to_test: Test knowledge of self-concept, self-identity, and social identity. Topics include the roles of self-esteem, self-efficacy, and locus of control, different types of identities (race/ethnicity, gender, age, sexual orientation, class), theories of identity development (gender, moral, psychosexual, social), and the influence of individuals (looking-glass self, role-taking), groups (reference groups), and culture on identity formation.

subcategory: 8B: Social thinking
title: Attributing Behavior and Prejudice
content_to_test: Test understanding of social thinking, focusing on attributional processes (fundamental attribution error, role of culture), how self-perceptions and environment shape perceptions of others, processes contributing to prejudice (power, prestige, class, emotion, cognition), stereotypes (self-fulfilling prophecy, stereotype threat), stigma, and ethnocentrism vs. cultural relativism.

subcategory: 8C: Social interactions
title: Elements of Social Interaction
content_to_test: Test knowledge of the structural elements of social interaction. Topics include status (achieved, ascribed), role (role conflict, role strain, role exit), groups (primary/secondary, in-group/out-group, group size/dyads/triads), networks, and formal organizations including the characteristics and perspectives on bureaucracy (iron law of oligarchy, McDonaldization).

subcategory: 8C: Social interactions
title: Self-Presentation and Social Behavior
content_to_test: Test understanding of self-presentation and social behavior. Topics include expressing/detecting emotion (role of gender and culture), impression management, the dramaturgical approach (front-stage vs. back-stage self), verbal/nonverbal communication, animal signals, attraction, aggression, attachment, altruism, social support, biological explanations of social behavior (foraging, mating, game theory, inclusive fitness), and individual vs. institutional discrimination.

subcategory: 9A: Understanding social structure
title: Sociological Theories and Institutions
content_to_test: Test knowledge of theoretical approaches to understanding social structure (microsociology vs. macrosociology, functionalism, conflict theory, symbolic interactionism, social constructionism, exchange-rational choice, feminist theory) and the major social institutions (education, family, religion, government, economy, health and medicine including medicalization and the sick role).

subcategory: 9A: Understanding social structure
title: Culture
content_to_test: Test understanding of culture, including elements of culture (beliefs, language, rituals, symbols, values), material vs. symbolic culture, culture lag, culture shock, assimilation, multiculturalism, subcultures and countercultures, mass media and popular culture, transmission, diffusion, and the evolution of human culture.

subcategory: 9B: Demographic characteristics and processes
title: Demographic Structure of Society
content_to_test: Test knowledge of demographic variables including age cohorts, the social construction of gender vs. sex, race and ethnicity (racialization, racial formation), immigration status, and sexual orientation.

subcategory: 9B: Demographic characteristics and processes
title: Demographic Shifts and Social Change
content_to_test: Test understanding of theories of demographic change (Malthusian theory, demographic transition), population pyramids, fertility/mortality rates, social movements, globalization, and urbanization.

subcategory: 10A: Social inequality
title: Social Stratification and Inequality
content_to_test: Test understanding of social inequality, including spatial inequality (residential segregation, environmental justice), aspects of social stratification (social class, SES, class consciousness, false consciousness, cultural/social capital, social reproduction, power/privilege/prestige, intersectionality), socioeconomic gradients in health, global inequalities, patterns of social mobility (intergenerational, intragenerational, vertical, horizontal, meritocracy), poverty (relative/absolute, social exclusion), and health/healthcare disparities.

subcategory: Foundations of Comprehension
title: Understanding and Inferring Meaning
content_to_test: Test the ability to understand the basic components of a text and infer meaning or intent from immediate sentence context. This includes identifying the author's thesis, main point, theme, recognizing the literal meaning of specific words, interpreting rhetorical devices, determining the overall purpose of a passage, and understanding tone.

subcategory: Reasoning Within the Text
title: Evaluating Arguments and Author Intent
content_to_test: Test the ability to integrate distant components of a text to infer an author's message, intent, purpose, belief, position, bias, and assumptions. Include recognizing and evaluating arguments, their structural elements (claims, evidence, support, relations), identifying contradictions, and assessing the credibility of sources and soundness of conclusions within the passage.

subcategory: Reasoning Beyond the Text
title: Application and Incorporation of New Information
content_to_test: Test the ability to apply or extrapolate ideas from the passage to new contexts or hypothetical situations, and assess the impact of incorporating new factors, information, or conditions on the ideas originally presented in the passage. This includes identifying analogous relationships and determining how new evidence might weaken, strengthen, or alter the author's central thesis.
